segments.s773-g17: A prepositional list functioning as the direct objects affected by the verb that follows. در is the preposition 'in/among' introducing the items; the nouns هیزم, گندم, گوسفند are coordinated with و (‘and’) and sit before the verb (a common poetic/fronted object order).
segments.s773-g18: ببست is a third-person past/imperfective narrative form meaning 'shut/closed' or 'cut off' and here takes the preceding list as its object. The phrase این برآورده چرخ بلند is the agent/topic: این 'this' + برآورده as a participial/nominal sense 'brought about' modifying چرخ بلند 'lofty sky/heaven' ( adjective follows noun ). Inversion places object before verb and the agent after, a poetic order.
segments.s773-g19: می‌آور is an imperfective/narrative verb here ('it brought/it kept coming' — habitual or continuing action in narrative). که introduces a result clause: از روزمان 'of our days' and بس نماند means 'no (enough) remained' — بس = 'enough' and نماند = 'was left/ remained', giving the sense 'so that nothing of our days was left.'
segments.s773-g20: چنین بود 'it was thus' is a simple past statement; تا بود is temporal 'as long as it was / while it lasted' (تا = 'until/while'); و connects the clauses. بر کس نماند means 'nothing remained for anyone' with بر marking the relation toward people (for/upon) and کس here meaning 'anyone/any person'. نماند is the past/resulting 'was left/ remained'.
segments.s773-g1: چنین is an adverb ‘thus/so’ placed before the predicate for emphasis. داد خوانیم is a verbal phrase where داد (’judgment/justice’) is used with خواندن to mean ‘to pronounce/declare judgment’; خوانیم is first‑person plural present (classical/epic present) ‘we proclaim’. بر یزدگرد is a prepositional phrase ‘against/upon Yazdegerd’ that marks the target of the proclamation.
segments.s773-g2: وگر = ‘but if / or if’ introduces an alternative clause. کینه خوانیم repeats the same verb خوانیم with the noun کینه (‘hatred, grudge’) as object, here meaning ‘we proclaim/utter hatred’ (or ‘we call it hatred’). ازین is composed of از + ین (= این) meaning ‘from/against these’; هفت گرد is a phrase ‘seven گرد’ (proper/name phrase) acting as the target of that hatred.
segments.s773-g3: اگر introduces a conditional clause. خود is emphatic ‘himself’. نداند is the classical present/gnomic form ‘does not know’ (negation built in), and همی here emphasizes ongoing or habitual sense. کین و داد are the two nouns serving as what he does not know: ‘hatred (revenge) and justice’. The sequence is conditional + subject + verbal negation + objects.
segments.s773-g4: مرا is the accusative ‘to me’ (here written as م + را): م is the cliticized object element and را is the definite/object marker placed after it; فیلسوف is the subject (‘the philosopher’). ایچ before پاسخ is an emphatic negative ‘nothing / not at all’, so ایچ پاسخ نداد = ‘gave (me) no answer at all’. نداد is past tense ‘did not give’. Word order is object – subject – negator – noun – verb (typical in older Persian clauses).
segments.s773-g5: وگر again introduces a hypothetical/conditional ‘and if’. The first گفت functions to introduce reported speech; the quoted clause is دینی همه بسته (‘religion is entirely closed’): دینی = ‘religious/of religion’, همه = ‘all/entirely’, بسته = ‘closed/sealed’. The final گفت repeats the verb ‘he said’ and closes the reported-speech frame — a common classical pattern where گفت ... گفت encloses what was said.
segments.s773-g6: بماند is a jussive/optative or stative form meaning ‘let it remain’ / ‘it remains’. همی is an emphatic/continuous particle here strengthening the verb. پاسخ is the object ‘the answer’, and اندر نهفت is a prepositional + adjectival phrase ‘hidden inside’ (اندر = ‘in/within’, نهفت = ‘hidden/concealed’). Together: ‘Let the answer remain hidden within.’
segments.s773-g7: اگر introduces a conditional. هیچ here means ‘any / at all’ (there is any). گنجست is an archaic contracted copula phrase (گنج + است) meaning ‘is treasure / there is treasure’. ای is the vocative particle ‘O’, and نیک رای is the vocative phrase ‘good-rayed’ i.e. ‘good-minded / good counselor’ (نیک = good, رای = counsel/opinion).
segments.s773-g8: بیارای here functions as an imperative/hortative ‘adorn/arrange (it)’ (an older form of آراییدن with a prefix). و = ‘and’. دل را is the object ‘the heart’ with را as the definite/object marker. به فردا مپای is a prohibitive clause: به = ‘to/on’, فردا = ‘tomorrow/the future’, and مپای is a negative jussive/imperative ‘do not rest/attach (your heart)’ (م- negative prefix + پای ‘to lean/rely’). So: ‘Adorn it, and do not fix your heart on tomorrow.’
segments.s773-g9: که is the conjunction 'that/for' introducing this clause. گیتی is the subject 'the world'. همی is the classical verbal prefix marking continuous/habitual aspect (here giving a sense of 'will/does'). The first بر is the preposition 'upon' governing تو ('you'), and the second بر functions as a prefixed particle with the verb بگذرد to form the compound sense 'pass over'; بگذرد is the present/subjunctive form meaning 'to pass'. The word order is literary: subject (گیتی) before the verbal phrase 'همی ... بگذرد'.
segments.s773-g10: زمانه is the noun 'time/the age' as subject. دم means 'breath' here used idiomatically as 'a moment' or 'a single life-breath'; ما is the possessive/oblique 'our' ('our breath(s)'). همی‌بشمرد is a fused classical present form meaning 'counts' or 'reckons' (همی- marking habitual/present). The clause literally reads 'time counts our breath(s)'.
segments.s773-g11: در خوردنت is an adverbial phrase 'in/with your eating' (خوردنت = خوردن + enclitic -ت 'your'). چیره کن is an imperative: چیره (dominant) + کن 'do/make' = 'make dominant' or 'master'. برنهاد is a verbal noun/compound 'place/set upon / establish' used as a complement, so the whole phrase instructs to 'establish mastery over your eating.'
segments.s773-g12: اگر is the conditional 'if'. خود is an emphatic pronoun 'yourself'/'oneself'. بمانی is the present/subjunctive form of ماندن 'to remain/last' ('if you remain/endure'). دهد is the present/future sense of 'to give' (here 'will give'). آنک is a classical relative/demonstrative 'that which/what', and داد is the past form 'gave' used nominally here to mean 'what was given'—together آنک داد = 'what was given'. The clause means 'if you yourself endure, (you) will get/receive what was given.'
segments.s773-g13: مرا is the possessive/oblique 'my' (here marking possession of the following nouns). دخل و خرج are the paired nouns 'income and expenditure'. ار (= اگر) is the conditional 'if'. برابر is the adjective 'equal', and بدی here is an archaic/poetic verbal form meaning 'you make' or 'you set'—so the phrase reads 'if you make my income and expenses equal.' Word order is literary: possessor (مرا) before the possessed nouns.
segments.s773-g14: زمانه is 'time/fortune' as subject. مرا (here the oblique/dative 'me' or 'to me') marks the person affected. چون is the comparative/simile 'like/as', برادر is 'brother'. بدی in this clause functions as a verbal copula/verb meaning 'treats' or 'behaves toward' in classical usage: 'time treats me like a brother.' The clause states the result of the previous conditional.
segments.s773-g15: تگرگ is the subject 'hail'. آمد is the past tense 'came'. امسال is the time adverb 'this year'. بر سان is a classical compound prepositional/simile 'in the manner of / like' (بر + سان), and مرگ is 'death' used as the thing being compared. The word order places the temporal adverb between verb and subject in a literary fashion.
segments.s773-g16: مرا is the dative/oblique 'for me'. مرگ is the subject 'death'. بهتر is the comparative adjective 'better'. بدی here functions as a classical verbal element meaning 'is/preferable' (i.e. 'seems better' or 'is better for'). از introduces the standard of comparison 'than'. تگرگ is the thing compared 'hail'. Put together: 'For me, death is better than hail.'

sentences.s773-s9: This lofty sky brought it about: it cut off the firewood, the wheat, and the sheep.
sentences.s773-s10: It kept coming so that nothing of our days remained; thus it was while it lasted, and nothing was left for anyone.
sentences.s773-s1: Thus we pronounced judgment upon Yazdegerd; or, if we proclaim hatred, we pronounce it against these seven Gerds.
sentences.s773-s2: If he himself does not know hatred and justice, the philosopher gave me no answer at all.
sentences.s773-s3: And if he said, ‘Religion is all closed,’ he replied, ‘Let the answer remain hidden within.’
sentences.s773-s4: If there is any treasure, O good counselor, adorn it, and do not set your heart on tomorrow.
sentences.s773-s5: For the world will pass over you, and time counts even our breaths.
sentences.s773-s6: Make mastery over your eating (i.e. restrain your appetite); if you yourself endure, you will receive what was given.
sentences.s773-s7: If you make my income and expenditure equal, time will treat me like a brother.
sentences.s773-s8: Hail came this year like death; for me, death is better than hail.
